name: Local: SSH or Telnet Detected on Non-Standard Port
notes: This rule triggers when a local SSH or Telnet communication is observed on a non-standard port. The default port for SSH and Telnet servers is TCP port 22 and 23.  Detecting SSH or Telnet operating on other ports may indicate an exploited host, where the attacker has installed these servers to provide backdoor access to the host.
conditions_text: when the flow context is Local to Local
when a flow matches all of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication

when the flow matches (APPLICATIONNAME(applicationid) = 'RemoteAccess.Telnet' AND destinationport != 23) OR (APPLICATIONNAME(applicationid) = 'RemoteAccess.SSH' AND destinationport != 22) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Local: SSH or Telnet Detected on Non-Standard Port on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the flow context is Local to Local
AND when a flow matches all of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication

AND when the flow matches (APPLICATIONNAME(applicationid) = 'RemoteAccess.Telnet' AND destinationport != 23) OR (APPLICATIONNAME(applicationid) = 'RemoteAccess.SSH' AND destinationport != 22) AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: SSH or Telnet Detected on Non-Standard Port

Event description: Detects an SSH or Telnet server on a non-standard port. The default port for SSH and Telnet servers is TCP port 22 and 23. Detecting SSH or Telnet operating on other ports may indicate an exploited host, where the attacker has installed these servers to provide backdoor access to the host.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
